title: Trump Extends Hormuz Deadline Again — New Hard Deadline April 6
detail: President Trump extended his Hormuz power-plant strike deadline a second time, setting a new hard deadline of Monday, April 6, 2026 at 8:00 PM ET for Iran to fully reopen the Strait. Trump posted on Truth Social on March 26: 'As per Iranian Government request... I am pausing the period of Energy Plant destruction by 10 Days to Monday, April 6, 2026.' He attributed the extension to Iran allowing 10 Pakistan-flagged oil tankers through the Strait as a goodwill gesture — 'they gave me ships.' Iran's government simultaneously denied having requested any extension and denied any negotiations are underway. Senior Iranian officials stated Tehran is in 'complete doubt' over Washington's willingness to genuinely negotiate. Trump separately told Fox News: 'I gave them a 10-day period... if they don't do what they have to do, I will knock out their power plants.' Despite the public extension, military operations continued uninterrupted.

title: IDF Strikes Ballistic Missile Sites in Kermanshah and Dezful; IRGC Navy Commander Killed
detail: The IDF conducted overnight strikes on dozens of Iranian military sites in western Iran, dropping approximately 70 bombs on ballistic missile storage and launch facilities in Kermanshah and Dezful. In the same operation, IRGC Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri — the senior officer who orchestrated Iran's drone blockade of the Strait of Hormuz — was killed in an Israeli airstrike on Isfahan. US CENTCOM confirmed the strike. Tangsiri was considered one of the key architects of Iran's Hormuz closure strategy, directing IRGC Navy operations against commercial shipping in the strait. IDF stated this brings total hits on designated operation targets to approximately 75%.

title: Iran Fires Two Missile Barrages at Central Israel in Retaliation
detail: Iran launched two separate rounds of ballistic missiles at central Israel in retaliation for the IDF's strikes in western Iran and the killing of IRGC Navy Commander Tangsiri. Israeli air defenses intercepted the majority of the incoming missiles, but some caused damage and injuries in central Israeli population centers. The barrages mark Iran's continued retaliatory capacity despite CENTCOM and IDF claims that Iran's missile production capability has been effectively destroyed. Iran's remaining arsenal consists primarily of pre-stockpiled missiles.

title: IDF Soldier Killed by Lebanese Rocket Fire
detail: One IDF soldier was killed and several others wounded by rockets fired by Iranian-backed fighters in Lebanon. The rocket fire targeted Israeli positions in northern Israel and along the southern Lebanese frontier. The death brings Israel's confirmed military fatality count from the conflict to 19+. IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir separately warned government ministers that the IDF is under 'severe strain' with manpower shortages of approximately 15,000 soldiers, while operating simultaneously on active fronts across Iran, Lebanon, Gaza, Syria, and the West Bank.

title: IDF Chief Zamir Warns of Severe Strain, 15,000 Troop Shortfall
detail: IDF Chief of Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir warned Israeli government ministers in a closed briefing that the IDF is under 'severe strain' — facing a manpower shortage of approximately 15,000 soldiers as it simultaneously prosecutes major combat operations on five fronts: Iran, Lebanon, Gaza, Syria, and the West Bank. Zamir's statement is the most significant public signal that Israel's military is being stretched by the scope of simultaneous operations. The warning comes as IDF planning reportedly calls for at least 3 more weeks of operations to systematically degrade Iran's defense industrial base, and as the 82nd Airborne's deployment to CENTCOM adds US ground force presence to the theater.

title: Pakistan Pushes for Direct US-Iran Talks in Islamabad
detail: Pakistan escalated diplomatic efforts on March 27, pushing for direct face-to-face US-Iran talks in Islamabad 'as early as Friday.' Pakistani PM Sharif's government is acting as the primary conduit for US-Iran indirect communication, having transmitted Trump's 15-point peace plan to Tehran. Iran's counter-proposal — demanding halt to all aggression, war reparations, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over Hormuz, and end to attacks on Hezbollah — remains far outside what Washington will accept. The ACLED assessment noted Iran is 'more likely to continue fighting than concede,' as the maximum Iran might offer falls short of the US minimum. Turkey is passing messages between both sides; Egypt has offered to host multilateral UN-backed talks.
